styled_description:
The diagram is a wide and flat horizontal pipeline, designed with a 16:9 aspect ratio, presenting a clean academic style suitable for NeurIPS. It is divided into two main conceptual regions: a "PCD Diagnosis" section on the left, characterized by warm tones, and a "Compile-Once Pipeline" section on the right, featuring cool tones.

The left region, labeled "PCD Diagnosis" in a prominent sans-serif font at its top center, is a large, subtly warm cream rectangular area. Within this region, three horizontally aligned components represent the stages of LLM probabilistic reasoning. On the far left is the "Parse" component, a medium-sized, horizontally elongated rounded rectangle with a subtle corner radius, filled with a very pale mint green and bordered by a thin, solid muted gray line. A small, playful, flat design checkmark icon in muted green is placed near its "Parse" label, with "82-100% accuracy" written below in a smaller sans-serif font. To its right, with moderate horizontal spacing, is the "Compute" component, a medium-sized, horizontally elongated rounded rectangle with a subtle corner radius, filled with a very pale coral. This component is visually emphasized as a bottleneck by a thick, solid soft coral border. A small, playful, flat design 'X' mark icon in soft coral is placed near its "Compute" label, with "22-78% accuracy" written below in a smaller sans-serif font. A thick, solid soft coral arrow, orthogonally routed and labeled "bottleneck" in bold soft coral text, connects the right side of "Parse" to the left side of "Compute", pointing right. Further to the right, completing the left section, is the "Decide" component, a medium-sized, horizontally elongated rounded rectangle with a subtle corner radius, filled with a very pale mint green and bordered by a thin, solid muted gray line. A small, playful, flat design checkmark icon in muted green is placed near its "Decide" label, with "100% accuracy" written below in a smaller sans-serif font. A thin, solid muted gray arrow, orthogonally routed, connects the right side of "Compute" to the left side of "Decide", pointing right.

The right region, labeled "Compile-Once Pipeline" in a prominent sans-serif font at its top center, is a large, subtly pale blue rectangular area. This section illustrates a solution pipeline flowing horizontally. On the far left of this region, slightly above the main flow, is the "3-5 Task Examples" component, a small, vertically oriented rounded rectangle with a subtle corner radius, filled with a very pale cream and bordered by a thin, solid muted gray line. A small, flat design input icon (an upward-pointing arrow into a rounded box) in muted gray is subtly placed near its label. A thin, solid muted gray arrow, orthogonally routed, points right and slightly down from the right side of "3-5 Task Examples" to the top-left side of the "LLM Inductor" component. The "LLM Inductor" is a medium-sized, horizontally elongated rounded rectangle with a subtle corner radius, filled with a very pale sky blue and bordered by a thin, solid muted gray line, positioned on the left side of the main flow. A subtle, light gray thought bubble motif is placed near its label. Below its "LLM Inductor" label, in smaller sans-serif text, reads "GPT-4o-mini, one-time $0.008 call". A thin, solid muted gray arrow, orthogonally routed, connects the right side of "LLM Inductor" to the left side of "TaskSpec". The "TaskSpec" component is a medium-sized, horizontally elongated rounded rectangle with a subtle corner radius, filled with a very pale sky blue and bordered by a thin, solid muted gray line, positioned to the right of "LLM Inductor". Below its "TaskSpec" label, in smaller sans-serif text, reads "declarative JSON intermediate representation describing task structure". A thin, solid muted gray arrow, orthogonally routed, connects the right side of "TaskSpec" to the left side of "Deterministic Compiler". The "Deterministic Compiler" component is a medium-sized, horizontally elongated rounded rectangle with a subtle corner radius, filled with a very pale sky blue and bordered by a thin, solid muted gray line, positioned to the right of "TaskSpec". Below its "Deterministic Compiler" label, in smaller sans-serif text, reads "7 core ops + 3 macros, same spec = same solver". A thin, solid muted gray arrow, orthogonally routed, connects the right side of "Deterministic Compiler" to the left side of "Verified Exact Solver". The "Verified Exact Solver" component, highlighted as the final output, is a medium-sized, horizontally elongated rounded rectangle with a subtle corner radius, filled with a soft steel blue and bordered by a thin, solid muted gray line, positioned on the far right of the region. A small, flat design output icon (a downward-pointing arrow out of a rounded box) in muted gray is subtly placed near its "Verified Exact Solver" label, with "100% accuracy, no LLM at inference time" written below in smaller sans-serif text. Below and slightly to the right of the "Deterministic Compiler" is a "3-Gate Verifier" component, a small, square-shaped rounded rectangle with a subtle corner radius, filled with a pale mint green and bordered by a thin, solid muted gray line. A thin, solid muted gray arrow, orthogonally routed, points downwards from the bottom of "Deterministic Compiler" to the top of "3-Gate Verifier". A thin, dashed muted gray arrow forms a feedback loop, starting from the right side of "3-Gate Verifier", curving upwards and then left, connecting to the bottom side of "TaskSpec". This feedback arrow is labeled "verify → refine if failed" in small, muted gray sans-serif text.

A crucial connecting element between the two main sections is a long, dashed muted gray arrow labeled "motivates" in a clear, muted gray sans-serif font. This arrow originates from the right side of the "Compute" component in the "PCD Diagnosis" section, extends horizontally across the central gap with orthogonal routing, and points to the left side of the "LLM Inductor" component in the "Compile-Once Pipeline" section.

Finally, at the very bottom of the entire diagram, spanning centrally across both left and right sections, is a prominent, bold, clean sans-serif tagline: "LLM contributes structure once → solver runs deterministically". The entire diagram uses clean sans-serif fonts and subtle, professional color fills, maintaining an elegant aesthetic.
caption: Figure 1: Overview. Left: PCD diagnosis decomposes LLM probabilistic reasoning into Parse, Compute, and Decide; computation is the dominant failure mode. Right: The compile-once pipeline induces a declarative TaskSpec from a few examples and compiles it into a verified exact solver.
original_description:
The diagram is a wide and flat horizontal pipeline, designed with a 16:9 aspect ratio, presenting a clean academic style suitable for NeurIPS. It is divided into two main conceptual regions: a "PCD Diagnosis" section on the left, characterized by warm and red tones, and a "Compile-Once Pipeline" section on the right, featuring cool and blue tones.

The left region, labeled "PCD Diagnosis" in a prominent sans-serif font at its top center, is a large, subtly warm beige rectangular area. Within this region, three horizontally aligned components represent the stages of LLM probabilistic reasoning. On the far left is the "Parse" component, a medium-sized, horizontally elongated rounded rectangle filled with a very pale green and bordered by a thin, solid dark gray line. A small, distinct green checkmark icon is placed near its "Parse" label, with "82-100% accuracy" written below in a smaller font. To its right, with moderate horizontal spacing, is the "Compute" component, a medium-sized, horizontally elongated rounded rectangle filled with a very pale red. This component is visually emphasized as a bottleneck by a thick, solid bright red border. A small, distinct red 'X' mark icon is placed near its "Compute" label, with "22-78% accuracy" written below in a smaller font. A thick, solid bright red arrow labeled "bottleneck" in bold red text connects the right side of "Parse" to the left side of "Compute", pointing right. Further to the right, completing the left section, is the "Decide" component, a medium-sized, horizontally elongated rounded rectangle filled with a very pale green and bordered by a thin, solid dark gray line. A small, distinct green checkmark icon is placed near its "Decide" label, with "100% accuracy" written below in a smaller font. A thin, solid dark gray arrow connects the right side of "Compute" to the left side of "Decide", pointing right.

The right region, labeled "Compile-Once Pipeline" in a prominent sans-serif font at its top center, is a large, subtly cool pale blue rectangular area. This section illustrates a solution pipeline flowing horizontally. On the far left of this region, slightly above the main flow, is the "3-5 Task Examples" component, a small, vertically oriented rounded rectangle filled with a very pale yellow and bordered by a thin, solid dark gray line. A small input icon (e.g., an upward-pointing arrow into a box) is subtly placed near its label. A thin, solid dark gray arrow points right and slightly down from the right side of "3-5 Task Examples" to the top-left side of the "LLM Inductor" component. The "LLM Inductor" is a medium-sized, horizontally elongated rounded rectangle, filled with a very pale blue and bordered by a thin, solid dark gray line, positioned on the left side of the main flow. Below its "LLM Inductor" label, in smaller text, reads "GPT-4o-mini, one-time $0.008 call". A thin, solid dark gray arrow connects the right side of "LLM Inductor" to the left side of "TaskSpec". The "TaskSpec" component is a medium-sized, horizontally elongated rounded rectangle, filled with a very pale blue and bordered by a thin, solid dark gray line, positioned to the right of "LLM Inductor". Below its "TaskSpec" label, in smaller text, reads "declarative JSON intermediate representation describing task structure". A thin, solid dark gray arrow connects the right side of "TaskSpec" to the left side of "Deterministic Compiler". The "Deterministic Compiler" component is a medium-sized, horizontally elongated rounded rectangle, filled with a very pale blue and bordered by a thin, solid dark gray line, positioned to the right of "TaskSpec". Below its "Deterministic Compiler" label, in smaller text, reads "7 core ops + 3 macros, same spec = same solver". A thin, solid dark gray arrow connects the right side of "Deterministic Compiler" to the left side of "Verified Exact Solver". The "Verified Exact Solver" component, highlighted as the final output, is a medium-sized, horizontally elongated rounded rectangle, filled with a slightly darker, vibrant blue and bordered by a thin, solid dark gray line, positioned on the far right of the region. A small output icon (e.g., a downward-pointing arrow out of a box) is subtly placed near its "Verified Exact Solver" label, with "100% accuracy, no LLM at inference time" written below in smaller text. Below and slightly to the right of the "Deterministic Compiler" is a "3-Gate Verifier" component, a small, square-shaped rounded rectangle filled with a very pale mint green and bordered by a thin, solid dark gray line. A thin, solid dark gray arrow points downwards from the bottom of "Deterministic Compiler" to the top of "3-Gate Verifier". A thin, solid dark gray arrow forms a feedback loop, starting from the right side of "3-Gate Verifier", curving upwards and then left, connecting to the bottom side of "TaskSpec". This feedback arrow is labeled "verify → refine if failed" in small, dark gray text.

A crucial connecting element between the two main sections is a long, dashed dark gray arrow labeled "motivates" in a clear, dark gray font. This arrow originates from the right side of the "Compute" component in the "PCD Diagnosis" section, extends horizontally across the central gap, and points to the left side of the "LLM Inductor" component in the "Compile-Once Pipeline" section.

Finally, at the very bottom of the entire diagram, spanning centrally across both left and right sections, is a prominent, bold, clean sans-serif tagline: "LLM contributes structure once → solver runs deterministically". The entire diagram uses clean sans-serif fonts and subtle color fills, maintaining a professional and elegant aesthetic.
